title: Quick start guide to Aotearoa Data Explorer | Stats NZ
content:
## Quick start guide to Aotearoa Data Explorer
29 September 2025, 9:00am
This guide explains the basics of Aotearoa Data Explorer to help you find, view, or extract data.
Aotearoa Data Explorer is a free online tool that presents data in a searchable, flexible, and dynamic way. It is an interactive web browser interface where you can view, query, and download data.
Aotearoa Data Explorer works best in Chrome or Edge browsers.
Find data Filter what data you see in a table Change the layout of a table Save, share, or download a table More help
There are two ways to find data from the Aotearoa Data Explorer homepage :
- search for data by entering keywords in the search field on the homepage
- browse for data by expanding a topic under the 'browse by' option and selecting a subtopic.
You can also access tables directly from links shared by Stats NZ or others.
How to find data in Aotearoa Data Explorer on YouTube provides a video tutorial.
The search or browse results may show a long list of tables. Use the ‘Refine your search’ options on the left to narrow the list to only tables with data of interest.
For example, to find a table that includes ethnicity, expand ‘Ethnicity’ in the left panel and tick one of the ethnic groups. The search results will update to show only tables with the selected ethnic group.
To view a table, click its title in the search results.

## Filter what data you see in a table
The first view of a table is usually filtered by default. You can change this to show a different selection of data from a list of options.
How to view data in Aotearoa Data Explorer on YouTube provides a video tutorial.
Filters that can be added or removed are shown to the left of the table under ‘Refine your data selection’. To change what is shown on screen, you can select or deselect items from this list.
If a four-arrow icon is shown under the dimension name, then ‘Advanced selection mode’ is available. We recommend using this option, if available, as it lets you easily select or deselect multiple items at once. Clicking the icon opens a separate screen.
In the ‘Advanced selection mode’ screen you can Collapse All , Expand All , Select All , or Deselect All just above the list of items. Deselect All can be useful to clear all currently selected items before choosing what to include in a table.
Additional selection modes are available at the top of the screen:
- Single item lets you select or deselect one item at a time
- Item and all items directly below will select the item you click and all its subcategories from the next lowest level.
- Whole branch will select the item you click and all its subcategories from all lower levels.
- All items at the same level will select all items at the same level as the item you click.
Once you have made your selections, click ‘Apply’ at the bottom right to update and return to the table.
Note: If more than 8,000 cells are selected you will see a warning message above the table and some data points may be missing. Either reduce your selections to fewer than 8,000 data points, or download the table as a CSV file to view in other software (for example, Excel).

## Change the layout of a table
To rearrange the layout of your table, click ‘Layout’ above the table. Drag dimensions between columns, row sections, and rows as desired. Click ‘Apply layout’ at the bottom right to update the table with the new layout.

## Save, share, or download a table
If you want to return to your table in future or share the table with others, copy the URL from your web browser. This will keep any customisation you have applied.
Note: If too many items are included in a table, the URL may be too long. An error message above the table will let you know if this happens.
You can also download data in an Excel or comma separated values (CSV) file using the ‘Download’ icon above the table.
Download data in Aotearoa Data Explorer on YouTube provides a video tutorial.
You can download information in the following formats:
- Table in Excel ‒ download an Excel file with the same view of data as your Aotearoa Data Explorer table. If more than 8,000 cells are selected, a warning message will display above the table and some data points may be missing. In this case, use one of the CSV download options instead.
- Filtered data in tabular text (CSV) ‒ download a CSV file with only the data you selected for your Aotearoa Data Explorer table.
- Unfiltered data in tabular text (CSV) ‒ download a CSV file with all the available data for the table, not just your selection. Note: Very large files may take a long time, and files with more than one million data points will be too large for Excel.
- Unfiltered reference information in tabular text (CSV) – download metadata in a CSV file. Metadata includes information such as footnotes, definitions, and quality notes.
If you need more help using Aotearoa Data Explorer:
- Aotearoa Data Explorer user guide – detailed guide
- Aotearoa Data Explorer tutorial videos on YouTube – video help
- Aotearoa Data Explorer news and information page – updates about the tool and available data
- API Portal – for developers to register and find documentation about using an Application Programming Interface (API) to retrieve data
- Information Centre – contact us.
- Aotearoa Data Explorer 29 September 2025
- Aotearoa Data Explorer user guide 29 September 2025
